capabilities: Implement retry strategies using exponential and linear backoff to handle transient errors in knowledge graph systems, ensuring system stability and data consistency.

procedure.action: Initialize retry logic with exponential backoff using a base delay of 100ms, doubling the delay up to a maximum of 5 seconds.
Invoke linear backoff for non-critical operations with a fixed delay increment of 200ms, capping at 3 seconds.

Compose retry logic with a knowledge graph API client, ensuring retries are logged with structured logging for traceability by composing module interfaces via adapter pattern; assert input/output type contracts match before connecting; assert integration_error_count < 1 after contract validation.

Set up monitoring to alert if retry attempts exceed 10 within a 1-minute window, indicating potential system issues using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Test retry mechanisms using a simulated network failure environment to ensure correct backoff behavior and system recovery using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Implement exponential and linear backoff strategies for reliable retries in knowledge graph systems, ensuring robust error recovery and minimizing system overload.
title: Exponential and Linear Backoff for Knowledge Graph Error Recovery

failureConditions: Excessive retries lead to system overload; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.

Incorrect backoff configuration causes delayed recovery; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
Lack of logging results in untraceable retry attempts.
metrics: input/output type contracts match before connecting
successConditions: Simulated failures show retries with correct backoff timing
System logs contain structured entries for each retry attempt
Alerts trigger when retry thresholds are exceeded
